
======================================================================
PAGE: Adaia
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Adaia
======================================================================

“I will teach my child of humans like you. You're a light in this dark place.”
Character
Adaia
Race
Elf
Gender
Female
Affiliation
Tabris family
Location
Dungeons
Quests
The Game
Family
Cyrion Tabris
(husband)
City Elf
(child)
Soris
(nephew)
Shianni
(niece)
Voice
Lora Brovold
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Origins
-
Leliana's Song
Adaia
is the mother of the
City Elf Warden
.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Origins - Leliana's Song
This section contains spoilers for:
Leliana's Song
.
Adaia is encountered in the dungeons of the Arl of Denerim as one of
Commander Harwen Raleigh's
many victims. She is grateful to be released, telling
Leliana
she is "wonderful" and "a light in this dark place." She also promises to teach her child about humans like Leliana.
Dragon Age: Origins
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
By 9:30, Adaia has been dead for several years. She had been living in
Denerim
with her husband
Cyrion
and their child, whom she trained as either a
warrior
or a
rogue
. It is stated that Adaia came across as something of a trouble-maker. She was killed by humans, but no details are given. She is remembered by family friends
Dilwyn
and
Gethon
as 'beautiful and full of life... and a bit wild," and the
City Elf
Warden
's features are compared to and contrasted with hers in their ambient dialogue.
If questioned, Duncan reveals that he attempted to recruit Adaia into the
Grey Wardens
, but was convinced by
Valendrian
that it was better for her to remain with her family. As there was no
Blight
at the time and thus no immediate need for recruits, Duncan acquiesced to Valendrian's wishes.
Adaia's old dagger,
Fang
, may be gifted to a City Elf Warden by Cyrion, depending on choices made during the City Elf Origin storyline and the
Unrest in the Alienage
quest.
Quests
The Game
Quotes
(
to
Leliana
): "Girl, you are wonderful. Thank you."
Gallery
Side view of Adaia
References

======================================================================
PAGE: Adal Helmi
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Adal_Helmi
======================================================================

Character
Adal Helmi
Race
Dwarf
Gender
Female
Class
Warrior
Caste
Noble
Location
Orzammar Proving
Orzammar Diamond Quarter
Quests
Dwarf Noble Origin
Family
Lady Helmi
(mother)
Denek Helmi
(brother)
Nerav Helmi
(relative)
Voice
Jeannie Elias
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Adal Helmi
is a member of
noble
House Helmi
. She is the eldest daughter of
Lady Helmi
, who is the matriarch of the house. She is first encountered at
The Nobles' Feast
during the
Dwarf Noble Origin
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
She fights in the
Provings
honoring the first military commission of King
Endrin Aeducan
's second child. She is a
warrior
who favors a
dual-wielding
style of fighting, using what appear to be two daggers. If she fights a female Dwarf Noble, Adal will tell her right before the battle starts that she is an inspiration to all of those who live by the sword.
Adal can later be found in the
Diamond Quarter
, loitering near a building not far from the entrance. Although she prefers that Lord
Pyral Harrowmont
becomes
king
, she is willing to support
Bhelen Aeducan
should he become ruler.
Quests
Dwarf Noble Origin
Gallery
Adal Helmi loitering in the Diamond Quarter

Ademaro
is a
surface dwarf
peddling his wares in the
Orzammar Commons
during the
Dwarf Commoner Origin
.
Involvement
As a surfacer, Ademaro is considered
casteless
: he is required to wear a
brand
while selling wares in the Orzammar Commons. He disapproves of the casteless that remain in
Dust Town
.
The Warden
may talk with him about
dwarves
that live on the surface. He initially refuses to show his wares, however he eventually complies.

PAGE: Adria
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Adria
======================================================================

Character
Adria
Race
Human
(
Ghoul
)
Gender
Female
Class
Rogue
Rank
Lieutenant
Location
Vigil's Keep - Basement
Quests
Adria's Plight
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
Adria
is a
human
who served the
Howe family
at
Vigil's Keep
. According to
Nathaniel Howe
, she was like a mother to him.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
.
Upon finding a letter from her on an injured
mabari
in the
Keep
's
basement
,
the Warden-Commander
learns that Adria has taken refuge deep within the Keep; reading the letter initiates the side quest
Adria's Plight
.
When she is discovered, the Warden-Commander finds that she has been corrupted by the
darkspawn
taint
and has turned into a
ghoul
. She must be killed to end her suffering, regardless of Nathaniel's protests.
Quests
Adria's Plight
Notable loot
Ring of Mastery

Dragon Age: Origins
Adwen
is a servant working at the
Arl of Denerim's Estate
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
When the
City Elf
Warden breaks into the Arl of Denerim's Estate, Adwen knocks out a
human
cook before they can call the guards. Soon after he runs off before things get ugly.
Quests
City Elf Origin
Quotes
"You've no idea how long that shem's had it coming."
"Now, if you'll excuse me, I'm getting out of here before the storm hits."

======================================================================
PAGE: Ahren
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Ahren
======================================================================

Character
Ahren
Race
Dwarf
Gender
Male
Caste
No caste
(
surfacer
)
Location
Frostback Mountain Pass
Voice
H. Richard Greene
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Ahren
is a
surface dwarf
merchant located outside the gates of
Orzammar
, in the
Frostback Mountain Pass
.
Background
He cannot enter Orzammar because the city is on a deadlock after King
Endrin Aeducan
passed away and the
throne
remains empty.
Involvement
Ahren will reveal that he was born on the surface and that he makes trips back to Orzammar two times a year in order to restock. He also finds his job important, as he views the trade that the surface dwarves do as Orzammar's lifeline. For that reason, he also feels bitter about the fact that the
Assembly
is denying a
caste
to their surface
kin
; however, he is optimistic that this could change by the time his children grow up. Ahren also mentions that he never had
Stone sense
, in contrast to his father.
Even though he says he is a merchant, he does not sell
the Warden
any goods.
Trivia
Ahren, like
Liselle
in the
Denerim Market District
, is one of the few merchants who does not barter with the Warden.

======================================================================
PAGE: Aidan
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Aidan
======================================================================

“The city of Amaranthine owes you a great debt. Thank you, Commander.”
Character
Aidan
Title
Constable
Race
Human
Gender
Male
Location
City of Amaranthine
Quests
Law and Order
Smuggler's Run
Voice
Adam Howden
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
Aidan
is a constable of the city guard in the
City of Amaranthine
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
.
Upon arrival at Amaranthine's gates, a guard stops the
Warden-Commander
and companions to check their bags for smuggled goods. The Constable steps in and scolds the guard for accusing the Commander of the Grey and dismisses him. Constable Aidan tells the Warden-Commander that the situation with the smugglers is dire and asks the Commander to help the city guard.
If the Commander agrees to help Aidan, he gives the
Law and Order
quest. Completing even the first task of this quest changes how Aidan and the guardsmen treat the Commander; they are grateful for the Commander's help and praise him/her for going beyond the call of duty. However, if the Commander sides with the smugglers as part of the
Smuggler's Run
quest, Aidan refuses to speak further with the Commander.
Aidan greets the Warden-Commander's party as they arrive at the besieged city of Amaranthine. Aidan grimly informs the Commander that he believes the city is beyond saving. He suggests putting it to the flame, which would burn the attacking darkspawn, along with the surviving citizens.
If Amaranthine is saved and the Warden-Commander sides with the Amaranthine guard over the smugglers, the
epilogue
mentions that Aidan distributed the smugglers' goods to the ailing citizens, and remained on good terms with the Wardens, sometimes relying on them for support in matters of law and order.
Quests
Law and Order
Smuggler's Run
Notes
You can
steal
about 10
from him.

PAGE: Alarith
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Alarith
======================================================================

Character
Alarith
Title
Merchant
Race
Elf
Gender
Male
Location
Alarith's Store
,
Elven Alienage
Voice
Gideon Emery
Buy price
25%
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Alarith
is the only
merchant
in the
Denerim Alienage
.
Background
Alarith was a
slave
in the
Tevinter Imperium
before he fled with his family to
Ferelden
with a caravan. On the way, his parents and brother were killed during a bandit attack.
Dalish
elves drove the bandits away, and then scoured the caravan for loot, leaving Alarith's belongings alone when they saw he was a fellow elf. Alarith touts the Alienage as "everything that is worth fighting for" to a male Tabris during the
City Elf Origin
.
Involvement
During the City Elf Origin, Alarith will offer advice to a male Tabris if they visit him in his
shop
. During the
Unrest in the Alienage
quest, he will update
the Warden
on the present situation in the Alienage.
Unless the Warden is an
elf
, he will not trade goods until after the
Unrest in the Alienage
quest is completed and the elves are saved. If
Caladrius
took the elves with him or used them in the Blood Ritual, Alarith's shop will remain permanently inaccessible.
Trivia
In the
toolset
, Alarith is described as the owner of a dry-goods shop and an occasional smuggler.

======================================================================
PAGE: Aldous
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Aldous
======================================================================

“If I was truly a skilled tutor then I expect I would not encounter such insouciance in my pupils. You can look that up later.”
Character
Aldous
Title
Historian
Race
Human
Gender
Male
Quests
Human Noble Origin
Voice
Jim Cummings
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Aldous
is a historian and tutor at
Castle Cousland
. He appears in the
Human Noble Origin
story to administer
education
to the youths of the castle.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Aldous is encountered in the castle's library, tutoring two young squires. He can teach the Human Noble about their
family's history
and some of the history of
Ferelden
. If responded to negatively, he may mock the Human Noble for never seeming to learn anything he teaches them. If the player decides to teach the lesson and has at least 16 points into Cunning he will be impressed that some of his lessons seemed to have stuck. He'll fall asleep if spoken to again. Aldous is killed when
Arl Howe
's men attack
Highever
on the eve of the
Fifth Blight
.
Quests
Human Noble Origin
Quotes
"I'm glad some of my lessons don't disappear into that yawning chasm between your ears, young man/lady."

======================================================================
PAGE: Alec
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Alec
======================================================================

Character
Alec
Title
Sheepherder
Race
Human
Gender
Male
Location
Vigil's Keep - Throne Room
Quests
A Day In Court
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
Alec
is a farmer in the
Arling of Amaranthine
. He is arrested by Amaranthine soldiers while trying to rob the convoy for food for his starving family.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
.
Alec's case is presented in
A Day In Court
. According to the law, the theft from the crown is punishable by hanging while, if he stole from someone else, he would escape with flogging instead. The
Warden-Commander
can have Alec being hanged, flogged, have him join the army or have
Varel
decide. If Varel decides, he will have Alec hanged.
If Alec is hanged, it will be impossible for the Warden-Commander to gain the commoners' trust during
The Peasant Revolution
; if Alec is flogged, there are no future consequences. Furthermore, if Alec is conscripted into the army, according to the
epilogue
, he becomes one of the great heroes of the next age and eventually founds an order of knights while people would call him
Ser Alec the Valiant
.
Quests
A Day In Court

======================================================================
PAGE: Alfstanna Eremon
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Alfstanna_Eremon
======================================================================

Character
Alfstanna Eremon
Title
Bann
of Waking Sea
Race
Human
Gender
Female
Location
Gnawed Noble Tavern
Royal Palace
Quests
Lost Templar
The Landsmeet
Family
Irminric Eremon
(brother)
Voice
Hellena Taylor
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Alfstanna Eremon
is the
Bann
of Waking Sea.
Background
Alfstanna's brother,
Irminric
, was the heir of the bannorn. However, he believed that his sister was a better ruler than he could ever become, so Alfstanna became the Bann and he joined the
Templar Order
.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Origins
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Once
the Landsmeet
starts, Alfstanna can be found in the
Gnawed Noble Tavern
conversing with Arl
Leonas Bryland
.
Alfstanna is involved in the quest
Lost Templar
—her brother
Irminric
is held captive in the
dungeons
of the
Arl of Denerim's Estate
suffering from
lyrium
withdrawal and asks
the Warden
to give his ring to his sister to prove his survival. If the Warden delivers the ring, Alfstanna will support the Warden during the Landsmeet.
If the Warden fails to win the Landsmeet but has her support, or alternatively wins the Landsmeet, has her support and opts to attack Loghain anyway, she will join the fight with Loghain and his guards in support of the Warden if it goes on long enough. In the event of a brawl, Alfstanna will utilize dual blades during the fighting.
Like
Bann Sighard
, she will recognize a
Human Noble
warden as
Bryce Cousland
's youngest child.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
A letter
found by the
Inquisitor
at
Caer Oswin
reveals that Bann Loren remains deeply affected by the loss of Landra and Dairren, having become increasingly reclusive over the years. In the letter, Bann Alfstanna expresses concern over Loren's "
strange new guards
," noting that the last time she was there, Loren was pale, almost delirious and that she fears the worst as Loren has not been seen in the months since.
Quests
Lost Templar
The Landsmeet
Quotes
Waking Sea heraldry
"Take this; it has been in my family for generations. Let it be a sign of the friendship between
Highever
and Waking Sea."
"Waking Sea stands with the Grey Warden!"
Dialogue
Warden
:
The Blight
is the threat here, not
Orlais
!
Alfstanna
: There are enough refugees in my bannorn now to make
that
abundantly clear!
Codex entries
Caer Oswin

Quests

Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Alimar
is a
casteless
dwarf
who is illegally acting as merchant in the
Orzammar
's
Dust Town
. He can be found in his shop,
Alimar's Emporium
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
During
Entering Jarvia's Hideout
, he is a potential source of information.
The Warden
can bribe him (price is 30
10
) to learn more things about the
Carta
, however he doesn't say anything important. If the Warden forces him to accept only 10 silvers, he will close the shop for the Warden and refuse to talk. It is also possible to kill him.
Once the
Finger Bone Token
is acquired the Warden can show it to him in order to acquire more information but he will only mention that he has seen
Jarvia
's men carrying them.
Quests
Entering Jarvia's Hideout

======================================================================
PAGE: Allison
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Allison
======================================================================

“Barlin wants to p-p-poison his traps.”
Character
Allison
Title
Villager
Race
Human
Gender
Female
Location
Lothering
Quests
Traps are a Girl's Best Friend
Voice
Kath Soucie
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Allison
is a widowed villager of
Lothering
. All strangers scare her and she is very intimidated by
the Warden
.
Involvement
Allison offers the quest
Traps are a Girl's Best Friend
. She wants to put traps on her lands to protect them from the coming
darkspawn
. She claims
Barlin
has all the village's traps in his fields, even though he sells them in the tavern.
Quests
Traps are a Girl's Best Friend
Notes
Allison will refuse to converse with
the Warden
if the party has failed a
pickpocketing
attempt in Lothering.
Trivia
According to her character file in the
toolset
, Allison was originally intended to be a potential
romantic encounter
for the male Warden.
However, this was never implemented.
References

======================================================================
PAGE: Alma

Alma

Female

Chantry of Our Lady Redeemer
,
City of Amaranthine
Quests

Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
Alma
is can be found in the
Chantry of our Lady Redeemer
in the
City of Amaranthine
.
Involvement
Alma is found praying for her husband's return. Attempting to talk to her will open the
Till Death Do Us Part
quest.
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
.
Once the relevant notes have been found,
The Warden-Commander
will find Alma's husband's corpse hanging from the ceiling of a house he wished to restore for them. His suicide note reveals that he killed himself as he believed he was unworthy of his wife. Alma will run off, distraught.
Quests
Till Death Do Us Part

======================================================================
PAGE: Aneirin
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Aneirin
======================================================================

“My home is out here, in the forest with the trees and animals. I learned to listen to them, and they taught me well.”
Character
Aneirin
Race
Elf
Gender
Male
Class
Mage
Affiliation
Circle of Magi
(formerly)
Dalish
(formerly)
Location
East Brecilian Forest
Quests
Wynne's Regret
Voice
Kirk Thornton
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Aneirin
is an
elven
mage
. He is a healer and a former apprentice of
Wynne
. He is the character
the Warden
will seek in the
East Brecilian Forest
for Wynne's personal quest,
Wynne's Regret
. Though he is not living with the
Dalish
, preferring living amidst the trees and the animals, he however is never far from their
camp
.
Background
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Aneirin was born in the
Denerim
alienage
but was taken to the
Circle
after his magic manifested. He was very defiant towards humans, especially those in a position of authority. In
Kinloch Hold
, he was apprenticed to
Wynne
, at the time a young and inexperienced teacher. Aneirin needed time to adapt to the Circle but Wynne gave him no such time. Whenever he tried to talk to her, she insisted that he concentrate on casting his spells. He often talked about the Dalish and how he wanted to seek them out. One night, at the age of fourteen, he fled from the tower.
Using his
phylactery
, the
templars
hunted him down and left him for dead. Fortunately, a Dalish clan found him, took him and nursed him back to health.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
After
Wynne
tells
the Warden
the story of how she failed Aneirin, they can ask around the
Dalish Camp
for information about him and are told that Aneirin can be found in the
East Brecilian Forest
. When Wynne and Aneirin are reunited, she apologizes for being a poor mentor, but he reveals that he harbors no ill feelings towards hers, that it is the past and she should put it behind her as well. Aneirin came to accept his path as a solitary
apostate
since he believes he would not have fit in with the Circle's templars and its
Chantry
. Wynne tells Aneirin that
Irving
could find a way for him to come back to the Circle and that he could change the Circle for the better. Aneirin admits he really liked Irving and that maybe he would consider her offer, but he is unwilling to commit to her proposal for the time being. If the Warden is a mage, he also suggests that the Warden could be a good candidate to rebuild the Circle. He gives Wynne
Aneirin's Token
.
After Wynne's quest, he remains at his campfire in the Eastern Brecilian Forest. He explains that he doesn't feel like being part of the Circle or the Dalish, preferring to be alone in the forest. He can also heal the party's wounds.
Quests
Wynne's Regret
Notes
Aneirin only appears in the forest after Wynne's quest is started, otherwise, only his camp fire will be here.
Aneirin is one of the people who appears when Wynne's
Amulet of Memories
is used.
References

PAGE: Anor Beraht

======================================================================

“You keep your head down and say 'aye' to any job I decide is low enough for scum like you.”
Character
Beraht
Title
Carta
leader
(informally)
Race
Dwarf
Class
Warrior
Rank
Elite
Caste
Merchant
Affiliation
Carta
Location
Dust Town Home
Beraht's Shop
Beraht's Hideout
Quests
Dwarf Commoner Origin
Voice
Pat Fraley
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Heroes of Dragon Age
Dragon Age II
(mentioned)
Anor
Beraht
is the leader of the
Carta
, a powerful criminal organisation, on the eve of the
Fifth Blight
. He is a member of the
Merchant caste
and legally owns a
shop
in the
Commons
.
He is encountered in the
Dwarf Commoner Origin
.
Background
Beraht employs many
casteless
dwarves
, who generally join his ranks out of desperation. The Dwarf Commoner and
Leske
work for Beraht as hired muscle, intimidating and killing those who cross their boss. Beraht has also invested money into women like Elsye and
Rica Brosca
, the Dwarf Commoner's half-sister, to turn them into
noble hunters
–women who try to seduce
noble
men and bear them a son. As a son would take on his noble father's caste, with the mother and her immediate family moving to the noble's estate to care for the child, Beraht hopes that one of his noble hunters will be successful in order to elevate his status alongside them by passing himself off as the child's uncle.
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
.
Under Beraht's leadership, the Carta once stole a gold statuette of
House Bemot
's
Paragon
and forced
Sigrun
to hide the stolen item in
Mischa
's
shop
, where she worked at the time. Sigrun agreed after Beraht threatened to have Mischa killed if she refused to do his bidding. The statuette was eventually discovered; Mischa was blamed for the theft and exiled to the
surface
.
Beraht also has strong ties with several
Mining
families
and thus gets a share in the profits of all the
lyrium
that is exported from
Orzammar
.
Jarvia
, his top lieutenant in the Carta, is also his lover.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Beraht tasks both the Dwarf Commoner and Leske with dealing with a
smuggler
that's holding out on him. Once that is dealt with, Beraht tasks them with rigging a
Glory Proving
by drugging Mainar to secure victory for
Everd
because
Lord Vollney
has made an illegal bet of 100
on Everd and works with Beraht to make sure that Everd wins the match.
Complications arise when the two casteless dwarves find Everd profoundly inebriated and irretrievably horizontal, forcing the Dwarf Commoner to disguise themself as Everd and take his place in the arena. Though "Everd" wins the matches and reaches the finals, the duplicity is subsequently exposed, leading to a massive scandal while the Proving is declared invalid. Lord Vollney loses a hundred sovereigns on the bet, and an angry Beraht orders
Jarvia
to rebuke Leske and the Dwarf Commoner as they languish in prison.
The downtrodden duo however manages to escape from their cells and confront Beraht in his chambers, slaying the crime-lord while leaving the Carta temporarily with no leader.
If the
protagonist
is not of Dwarf Commoner Origin, however, it is unclear how Beraht died as
Leske
can still be found locked up in his cell when they visit Orzammar as part of
A Paragon of Her Kind
quest.
Quests
Dwarf Commoner Origin
Skills
Beraht in
Heroes of Dragon Age

Shield Defense
Shield Cover
Quotes
Warden:
"I was kind of hoping to spend time with my sister."
Beraht:
"Aw, that's touching. Wait, I forgot; I don't care."

PAGE: Anora

“We have been given the gift of freedom by our forbearers. Let us not squander it.”
Character
Anora

Appearances
Dragon Age: The Stolen Throne

Dragon Age II

Dragon Age: Redemption
Heroes of Dragon Age
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Trespasser
(mentioned)
Anora Theirin
(née Mac Tir) is the wife of King
Cailan Theirin
and daughter of Teyrn
Loghain Mac Tir
. She is the
Queen
of
Ferelden
, a consummate ruler and committed to ensuring economic security for Ferelden.
Background
Anora was born to the Teyrn of
Gwaren
and his wife, a former cabinetmaker named
Celia
. She spent much of her early life in Gwaren with her mother while her father stayed half the year at court. In 9: 10
Dragon
, after Celia argued that Loghain was spending too much time away from his daughter, Loghain started taking her to court.
There she met and was betrothed to the young
Prince Cailan
. As a child, Anora began to display many characteristics which did not fade in adulthood: she was imperious and domineering, refusing to play with other children and leading her betrothed around like a puppy on a leash. Yet she also exhibited charm, intelligence and a fierce sense of independence. Her youth was also marked by significant rebellion, as illustrated by the
West Hills
incident, which involved Anora and Cailan running off to the West Hills arling in 9:23 Dragon to help a freeholder rid his lands of
giants
.
Anora eventually married Prince Cailan and though the two were fast friends, the marriage was initially one of convenience rather than true love. Though that friendship eventually blossomed into love, Anora always viewed her husband as a fool, lovable and charming, but a fool nonetheless.
Upon Cailan's accession, Anora assumed most of the monarchical duties her husband shirked. She did not rule in her own name, but there was no doubt who really ruled
Ferelden
. To her credit, Anora was held in much higher esteem than her husband by the people of Ferelden, nobility and commoners alike. She also commanded the respect of foreign nations, having once inspired
Empress Celene
of
Orlais
to declare, "Anora of Ferelden is a solitary rose among brambles."
Despite their five years of marriage, Anora and Cailan did not have any children which lead to rumors that she was barren.
Anora's strongest bond was with her father, whom she viewed as a role model and as someone who knew her better than anybody in the world. Indeed, one reason why Anora finds so many unimpressive is that they inevitably fail to live up to her exceptional standard of her father.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Origins
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Anora leading the
Fereldan
army at the
Battle of Denerim
.
Anora is deeply saddened by the demise of her husband at the
Battle of Ostagar
, though she stands beside her father as he proclaims himself regent. She continues to perform the duties of a ruler, seeing to the day to day administration of the realm, though for the first time cracks began to emerge between her and her father. According to Anora's servant,
Erlina
, Anora eventually tried to get the truth out of Loghain, strongly suspecting that it was in fact he that had been responsible for Cailan's death. However, Loghain only deflected her inquiries. Furthermore, as the
Fereldan Civil War
and
Fifth Blight
exacerbate the state of the country, Anora begins to believe her father may be misguided but he is too proud to admit it. Anora eventually visits
Arl Rendon Howe
in his
Denerim estate
and demands the truth regarding the dark rumors pertaining her father.
Soon after
the Warden
and
Arl Eamon
of
Redcliffe
's arrival in
Denerim
, Arl Rendon Howe, Loghain's closest lieutenant, accuses Anora of treason and imprisons her in his
estate
. Anora dispatches her servant Erlina in secret to meet with Arl Eamon and the Warden—and also to ask for their help. The Warden storms Howe's estate and rescues Anora, but they were stopped by Ser
Cauthrien
and a company of Loghain's guards. If the Warden reveals their intention to rescue Anora, she will deny it and accuse the Warden of kidnapping her. Anora would later justify her ruse to the Warden by explaining she was trying to discreetly evade confinement by her father's henchmen and they threatened to draw her unwanted attention by announcing her to Loghain's most trusted lackey. Ultimately the Warden is either captured by Cauthrien and has to be rescued/escape from the prison of
Fort Drakon
or fights their way out of Howe's estate. In the meanwhile, Anora flees to Arl Eamon's estate and can be the one that informs Eamon of the Warden's capture. Once the Warden returns to Eamon's estate, Anora begins to scheme against her father. Anora was the first to suggest that the Warden should investigate the
Elven Alienage
in Denerim, as she has suspicions that Loghain may be behind the unrest there.
Anora attempts to sway the Warden to support her claim to the Ferelden throne, arguing they would look to the public as supporting Ferelden's best interests rather than solely the
Grey Wardens
. Furthermore, despite
Alistair
's
Theirin
blood, Anora also fears Alistair will make a poor ruler − just like his brother Cailan.
If the Warden talked to Anora before
the Landsmeet
, promised to support her claim for the throne and didn't insist that Loghain must pay for his crimes, she speaks in favor of the Grey Wardens. If the Warden earned Anora's support, Anora paints Loghain as a villain in front of the Landsmeet and paints the Warden as her rescuer from Howe's dungeons. Otherwise she will speak against them, claiming they are slandering Loghain and she also accuses Alistair of being an impostor and an usurper. She will also chastise the Warden for refusing to cooperate with her and accuses them of being the true threat to Ferelden.
Ultimately, the Warden wins the Landsmeet. If Loghain is not executed for his crimes,
Riordan
will propose that Loghain make amends by becoming a
Grey Warden
. Anora supports her father's redemption and tries to persuade the Warden to admit Loghain into the Grey Warden order by arguing he will either be a valuable asset as a renowned general, or die from the
Joining
.
If Anora is made queen:
Prior to the Landsmeet, Anora negotiates an agreement with the Warden: the Warden would support her bid for the throne and in return she would rally the nobility against her father. Both fulfill their end of the bargain, though Anora strongly protests the proposed execution of her father following her downfall. Nevertheless, the nobility proclaim her queen, her first act being to appoint the Warden as commander of the Ferelden Coalition. She is present at the
Battle of Denerim
, rallying her troops before they attack the
darkspawn
.
Following the defeat of
Urthemiel
, Anora is crowned by
Grand Cleric
Elemena
. She proclaims the Warden Hero of Ferelden and strips the
Howe family
of
Amaranthine
, granting it to the
Grey Wardens
. She goes on to refill Ferelden's empty coffers with new trade deals and establishes a university.
If Anora marries Alistair:
Prior to the Landsmeet, Anora enters negotiations with the Warden. It is agreed that the Warden would support Anora's bid for the throne on the condition that she would marry
Alistair
. Anora is hugely uncomfortable with this suggestion, but reluctantly agrees to it, adding the caveat that the two would marry and rule on equal terms - there would be no repeat of Cailan's reign.
If Loghain is spared and becomes a Grey Warden, then an infuriated Alistair uses his betrothal to Anora and status as co-ruler of Ferelden to leave the Grey Wardens and the Warden's party. While Alistair sulks, Anora appoints the Warden as the commander of the Fereldan Coalition forces. Anora eventually rallies the Fereldan forces during the Battle of Denerim.
If Loghain is executed for his crimes, then Anora is dismayed by her father's death but soon composes herself. Anora asserts that King
Maric Theirin
would have wanted the Theirin line to be tied to the Mac Tir family and would have approved of her and Alistair's union. While Alistair fulfills his duties as a Grey Warden and faces the Blight, Anora elects to remain in Denerim and rule in Alistair's stead.
The accession and betrothal of Anora and Alistair was formally recognized by the Landsmeet, with the couple being crowned and married following the defeat of
Urthemiel
. Though the marriage was not a happy one for either party, the pair prove popular with the people.
If Anora marries the (male Human Noble) Warden:
Prior to the Landsmeet, Anora enters negotiations with the Warden. Anora agrees to rally the nobility against her father in return for the Warden's support in her bid for the throne. The Warden, however, agreed to support her only if she would marry him following the end of the
Blight
. This proposal surprises Anora, though she eventually agrees. She was proclaimed queen by the Landsmeet, where she announces her engagement to the Warden and also appoints him commander of the Ferelden Coalition. She is present at the
Battle of Denerim
, rallying her troops before they attacked the
darkspawn
. 
Following the defeat of
Urthemiel
, Anora was crowned by Grand Cleric Elemena. She proclaims her husband-to-be Hero of Ferelden and Prince-Consort, marrying him six months following her coronation. Many declared that if the two did not end up vying for control of the throne, they would usher in a golden age for Ferelden.
If Anora is deposed:
Despite her efforts to win the throne,
Alistair
is proclaimed king before the Landsmeet. However Anora refuses to recognize Alistair as her king. Some see her as a threat and call for her execution, but Alistair shows clemency and imprisons her instead.
Despite her downfall, she remains as the heir of King Alistair if he should perish from the Blight. If Alistair makes the ultimate sacrifice while slaying the Archdemon, she becomes the new Queen of Ferelden and she recognizes Alistair as the last person of the
Theirin
bloodline.
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
.
Anora in
Awakening
If Anora is the queen and rules alone:
Anora arrives at
Vigil's Keep
with a regiment of troops and the
templar
,
Rylock
, to inspect the newly established Warden stronghold in
Amaranthine
. She wishes
the Warden-Commander
luck, charging them with the rebuilding of Vigil's Keep and the restoration and administration of Amaranthine. She also approves the conscription of the
apostate
,
Anders
, into the Wardens' ranks, despite Rylock's protestations.
The Darkspawn Chronicles
This section contains spoilers for
:
Darkspawn Chronicles
.
It is revealed that Anora becomes engaged to Alistair as an effort to placate the Ferelden nobles and unite them after Arl Eamon reveals Alistair's true heritage. The battle of Denerim takes place before they can get married, forcing Alistair to march to defend Denerim and leading to his death and Ferelden's fall to the Blight.
Dragon Age: Redemption
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Redemption
.
The plot follows BioWare's own canon
, meaning it may not follow some of the player's choices in the games.
Anora rules
Ferelden
and is loved by her people. She has repaired the damages caused by the
Fifth Blight
, helping refugees and honoring the
Grey Wardens
for stopping the Blight.
Dragon Age II
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age II
.
If Anora is the queen:
Queen Anora offers to reinstate the commission of any Fereldan officer who fled in the Fifth Blight if they will return home and rejoin the army. This prompts veterans such as
Aveline Vallen
to consider returning to Ferelden.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Anora tarot card
Previously wife to
King Cailan
, descendant of
Calenhad
and last of the
Theirin
bloodline to rule
Ferelden
, Queen
Anora
came into her own during the
Fifth Blight
. With the throne left vacant following her husband's death, she was confirmed as sole ruler with the support of the
Hero of Ferelden
—and this confirmation allowed her to rally her nation behind the
Grey Wardens
and the battle that ultimately ended the war with the
darkspawn
before it ever truly began.
If
the Warden
was Anora's Prince-Consort...
After the Blight's end, Anora and the Hero of Ferelden were married. She began the process of rebuilding a country devastated by the Blight's corruption... a process that is considered to have gone well, at least until the Hero of Ferelden's disappearance from the Fereldan court several years ago.
Since that time, concerns linger that Queen Anora has still not produced an heir, concerns that she has thus far successfully dismissed. The people believe her rule wise and well-reasoned, and they fully supported her decision to give the
rebel mages
safe harbor in
Redcliffe
following an impassioned speech she delivered in
Denerim
six months ago. When the rebel mages took over both the town and castle, forcing
Arl Teagan
to flee for the capital, her support among the nobility dwindled rapidly. What this will mean for her rule in the coming years—particularly with renewed interest from neighboring
Orlais
—remains to be seen.
—
From
Queen Anora Mac Tir
If
Alistair
is King and Anora is Queen...
King Alistair
Theirin
was crowned the ruler of
Ferelden
after the end of the
Fifth Blight
; his people consider him a hero for fighting against the darkspawn as a
Grey Warden
. Few Wardens have ever left the secretive order, and he is the only of those who went on to rule a nation; Queen
Anora
, daughter of the legendary
Teyrn
Loghain
, rules at his side. Although Ferelden has prospered under their reign, it was greatly weakened by the
Blight
. Tensions with neighboring
Orlais
have been high, and many believe a renewal of their long-standing conflict is inevitable.
—From
Codex entry: Alistair (Inquisition)
If Anora is either Queen alone or alongside King Alistair:
Anora allows the
rebel mages
to take refuge in
Redcliffe Castle
. However after the rebel mages are aggressively taken over by the
Tevinter
Magister
Gereon Alexius
, Alexius begins to have rebel mages displace the people of
Redcliffe
.
If
the Inquisitor
recruits the
mages
:
Having enough of the mages' abuse, Anora (alongside King
Alistair
if they rule jointly), arrives in Redcliffe with a contingent of soldiers shortly after the
Inquisition
's ousting of Alexius to inform the rebel mages that they have outstayed their welcome and recommends that they take whatever deal that
the Herald
offers, informing them that they will leave Ferelden no matter what.
If Anora is Queen alone:
Seeing the Inquisition's rising influence, Anora reaches out to the Inquistor to request aid in uprooting
Venatori
cultists in the Royal Palace at Denerim, hoping to avert a repeat of what happened at Redcliffe. With the aid of the Inquisition's agents, four Tevinter spies and a possible plot to assassinate the queen are discovered. Once the
Orlesian Civil War
is resolved with the help of the Inquisition, Anora writes for a second time to ask that they help facilitate peace talks in
Jader
between Ferelden and
Orlais
. The Inquisition sends
Josephine Montilyet
to moderate peace talks, and, despite years of bad blood between the two nations, the talks go smoothly thanks to Anora's skills as a diplomat and Orlais' desire to avoid further wars. In thanks, she sends the Inquisition a
sword
that once belonged to King
Calenhad
.
Trespasser
This section contains spoilers for
:
Trespasser
.
If Anora is Queen:
If Anora rules, whether alone, with Alistair, or the Warden, she sends a letter to Arl Teagan reaffirming his ability to make decisions at the
Exalted Council
on behalf of the Crown.

Codex entries

Trivia
In Bioware's character file for her, Anora's appearance is described as: "Beautiful. Slim."
Writer
Sheryl Chee
has said that when going out for a drink Anora would "skip the beer and go straight to tequila shots."
Writer
David Gaider
states that, "the most important thing to know about Anora is that she is her father's daughter."
When Anora is first seen by the party, she is wearing guard armor. The Warden may say "Aren't you a little short for a guard?" This is similar to what Princess Leia said upon first meeting Luke Skywalker in
Star Wars
, who was wearing Stormtrooper armor at the time. If a dwarf or elf uses this line, Anora points out the irony.

Character
Anwer Dace
Title
Lord
Race
Dwarf
Gender
Male
Class
Warrior
Caste
Noble
Location
Orzammar Diamond Quarter
,
Aeducan Thaig
Quests
A Paragon of Her Kind
A Prince's Favor: The First Task
Family
Lady Dace
(daughter)
Jerrik Dace
(distant cousin)
Brogan Dace
(distant cousin)
Voice
Michael Gregory
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The Golems of Amgarrak
(mentioned)
Anwer Dace
is a
dwarven
lord and the patriarch of
House Dace
, one of the major
noble
Houses
of
Orzammar
.
Background
As the leader of his House, Anwer has the final say on any decision concerning his family. He has appointed his daughter,
Lady Dace
, as House Dace's
deshyr
in the
Assembly
and trusts her enough to handle the house's business in his stead during his absence. Anwer is also known to employ mercenaries during his
Deep Roads
expeditions. He has contacts with
surface dwarf
merchants, and uses his influence with them to reward allies.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Anwer Dace is involved in the
A Prince's Favor: The First Task
, a sub-quest of
A Paragon of Her Kind
.
Lord Anwer will be on an expedition in the Deep Roads when
the Warden
first arrives in Orzammar during the main plot line. Should the player choose to support
Bhelen Aeducan
as the new
king
,
Vartag Gavorn
will give the Warden two forged promissory notes which show
Harrowmont
promised
House Dace
and
House Helmi
the same land for their support. Upon revealing this to Anwer's daughter, Lady Dace, she will send the Warden to seek out her father in the Deep Roads, where he and his mercenary guards can be found being swarmed by
deepstalkers
in
Aeducan Thaig
.
After saving him, the player can then show him the promissory notes, at which point Lord Anwer will assure the Warden that Bhelen has House Dace's vote in the Assembly. The Warden can also persuade Anwer to give him a personal reward, which results in a discount from
Gorim
in
Denerim
(requires at least one point in
Coercion
skill tree) or can also persuade him in changing his support to Prince Bhelen (requires Improved Coercion). This has an
impact
on the political influence that Bhelen will receive. Anwer will immediately return to Orzammar to authorize House Dace's change of allegiance and offers the Warden the opportunity to travel with him in safety, saving the player the trek back to a map exit.
Quests
A Prince's Favor: The First Task
A Paragon of Her Kind
Notes
He can be pickpocketed twice for two tier 3
Thorn of the Dead Gods
daggers
, an item which also wields a unique
codex entry
.
The BioWare wiki mentions that
Jerrik Dace
is the nephew of Lord Anwer Dace which contradicts this
codex entry
. Furthermore, it mentions that Mandar Dace is his son which also contradicts with the established in-game lore.
It is unknown which version is correct.
References

PAGE: Armaas
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Armaas
======================================================================

“The Qun is a lie!”
Character
Armaas
Title
Merchant
Race
Qunari
Gender
Male
Affiliation
Tal-Vashoth
Location
Silverite Mine
Vigil's Keep
(conditional)
Quests
The Righteous Path
Trade Must Flow
Buy price
23%
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
Armaas
is a
Tal-Vashoth
merchant working in the
Silverite Mine
. He is blatantly self-centered, willing to form trade contracts with anyone, even
darkspawn
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
.
If
the Warden-Commander
ask Armaas why he works for the darkspawn, he will say that he was caught by them but agreed to bring them supplies in return for gold. If you tell Armaas that he is in danger of getting corruption, he'll mention that they have promised him protection.
He is offended if you call him a Qunari, and is proud of his abandonment of the
Qun
. He stresses that he doesn't help darkspawn, but himself.
The Warden-Commander has also then the option of killing Armaas for working with the darkspawn.
Armaas can be recruited to trade at
Vigil's Keep
, thereby helping to complete the side quest
Trade Must Flow
if the Warden-Commander has already talked with
Mistress Woolsey
. Upon arrival at Vigil's Keep, Armaas can supply new items for sale to the other vendors.
Armaas also acts as a
merchant
to the Warden in the Silverite Mine, if he is asked to. He can be also persuaded (no coercion skills required) for a discount to which Armaas will agree to saying that he is not heartless.

Trade Must Flow

Quotes

Notes
Armaas initially sells his wares at 120% of their original price, after the discount however it is 105% of which is the lowest markup from any other merchant in
Awakening
.
Armaas is the only merchant in
Dragon Age: Origins
who does not buy items from the Warden for 25% of their original value, but instead for 23%, or 20% after the discount.
Bugs
His vendor page names this Tal-Vashoth as Armass instead of Armaas.
It is possible for him to appear at Vigil's Keep before you even head to the Silverite Mine. This does not affect the Trade Must Flow quest.
It is also possible that if you choose to kill Armaas in the Silverite Mine because of his deals with the darkspawn, he will still be in Vigil's Keep when you return later on despite his death.

For the merchant in Dragon Age II, see
Armor Stand
.
Character
Armor Merchant
Title
Merchant
Race
Dwarf
Gender

Caste

Quests

Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Armor Merchant
is a
dwarf
of the
Merchant caste
encountered during the
Dwarf Noble Origin
.
Background
This merchant was one of those who auctioned permits to sell their wares in
Orzammar
's
Diamond Quarter
as part of the
festivities
for the nomination of King
Endrin Aeducan
's middle son/daughter as the new Commander.
As his name suggests, he is mostly selling dwarven
armor
as well as
Metal Shards
.
When the Dwarf Noble talks to the Armor Merchant, he says how honored he is that his/her Highness visits him and admits that his wares cannot be matched with the amazing
Aeducan armor
that the Dwarf Noble is wearing but if they are interested he could present his wares. However,
Gorim
disrupts him, saying that he knows very well that the
House Aeducan
has its own
royal
armorers, to which the merchant quickly apologizes. If the Dwarf Noble pursues for more information regarding his wares, he will mention that he personally purchases the best iron ore and oversees the armor's crafting by the most skillful of the
Smith caste
. Regardless of dialogue choice, however, the Dwarf Noble can afterwards take a look at his wares.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
When the quest
A Noble Expedition
ends, all the items are removed from your inventory (except for the
DLC
items), including any items you may have purchased from this merchant.

Character
Ash Warrior Leader
Title
Ash Warrior
Race
Human
Gender
Male
Location
Ostagar
Voice
Greg Ellis
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The
Ash Warrior Leader
commands the
Ash Warriors
, a group of
Berserker
warriors
stationed at
Ostagar
. He does not care much for
elves
, but expresses admiration towards
dwarves
.
Involvement
He is preparing his men to scout the
Wilds
and track the progression of the
darkspawn
horde on the eve of the
Battle of Ostagar
.
The Warden
may ask him about the Ash Warriors, their
mabari
war dogs, and their activities. He will also explain the origin of his men's fighting style and tell the story of
Luthias Dwarfson
to a non-dwarven Warden.
It is unknown if he survived the
Battle of Ostagar
, but some bandits can tell the Warden that they've seen a group of wounded Ash Warriors passing near
Lothering
.

PAGE: Ashalle
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Ashalle
======================================================================

Character
Ashalle
Race
Elf
Gender
Female
Affiliation
Sabrae clan
Location
Dalish Camp
Quests
Dalish Elf Origin
Voice
Kath Soucie
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Ashalle
is the guardian of the
Dalish
Elf
Warden
, and is only encountered in the
Dalish Elf Origin
.
Background
Ashalle was a friend of the Dalish Warden's parents. After
the Warden
's father was slain and their mother disappeared, Ashalle raised the Warden among the
Sabrae clan
under
Marethari
's guidance.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Origins
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
After awakening from a coma, the Warden can speak with Ashalle at the Dalish Camp. The Warden can
persuade
her into finally telling them about their parents, and will provide a key to a chest containing an
Heirloom Necklace
.
She is encountered again at the Post-Coronation party in the
Royal Palace
, if the Warden survived the battle with the
archdemon
. If the Warden asked for land for Dalish, she's surprised it happened and joyful to no end.
Quests
Dalish Elf Origin
Trivia
Ashalle is not with Marethari's clan during
Dragon Age II
because she is still in Ferelden. If the Warden is of Dalish Elf origin, Ashalle is managing the Dalish boon.

======================================================================
PAGE: Athras
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Athras
======================================================================

Character
Athras
Race
Elf
Gender
Male
Class
Rogue
Affiliation
Dalish
Location
Dalish Camp
,
Brecilian Outskirts
Quests
Lost to the Curse
Family
Danyla
(wife)
Unnamed Daughter
Voice
Mark Meer
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Athras
is a
dalish elf
found in the
Dalish Camp
along with the rest of his clan.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
He mentions that he wants to go in the forest, but
Zathrian
has forbidden his clan to venture in there. If
the Warden
is not of
Dalish origin
, he will be reluctant to share the reasons why he wants to go there, however with enough
Coercion
(two points are required) he will be convinced. Athras will mention that he and
Danyla
fought alongside each other during the initial
werewolf
attack. Danyla was wounded, and although
Zathrian
insists that she died of her injuries, Athras does not believe this explanation. Instead, he fears she may have become a werewolf, as Zathrian refused to let him see his wife's corpse. After the discussion is over, the quest
Lost to the Curse
will be activated.
Upon returning to the Dalish Camp and informing him of this news, the Warden receives
Athras's Pendant
as a token of his gratitude.
If the Warden ignores Danyla and chooses to side with the werewolves, Athras flees the clan and will seek out Danyla. He begs her for the curse so that they can finally be with one another.
Quests
Lost to the Curse

PAGE: Augustine
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Augustine
======================================================================

“I've served the Chantry longer than she has, and what do I get?”
Character
Augustine
Title
Mother
Race
Human
Gender
Female
Affiliation
Orlesian Chantry
Location
Fort Drakon
Quests
Captured!
Voice
Carolyn Seymour
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Mother
Augustine
is a member of the
Chantry
at the
Denerim
Cathedral.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Mother Augustine can be found praying in Fort Drakon's chapel. If
Leliana
and
Morrigan
talk to her, she'll mention that she wasn't expecting them and asks whether Mother
Boann
sent them. She'll tell them that she could have handled work in Fort Drakon perfectly well. If Leliana calms her down, Augustine will say that she served the Chantry longer than Boann, and that she didn't spend thirty years ministering to the condemned to retire and spend her days tatting lace. She leaves to give Boann a piece of her mind.
If the Warden assumes the identity of new recruits, Augustine will be busy praying and won't interact with them.
If Sten tries to talk to her she becomes scared, calling him a giant, and yells for the guards.
Quests
Captured!

PAGE: Aura
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Aura
======================================================================

Character
Aura
Race
Human
Gender
Female
Location
Vigil's Keep
Chantry of our Lady Redeemer
,
City of Amaranthine
Quests
Justice for Kristoff
Family
Kristoff
(husband)
Unnamed sister
Unnamed nephew or niece
Appearances
Dragon Age: Warden's Fall
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
(mentioned)
Aura
is the wife of
Kristoff
, one of the
Grey Wardens
assigned to
Ferelden
in the wake of the order's re-establishment after the
Fifth Blight
.
Background
According to the
letters she sent
to Kristoff, she, her sister, and her sister's child lived in
Jader
, an
Orlesian
Grey Warden base.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
.
Once
Shadows of the Blackmarsh
has been completed, Aura will travel to
Vigil's Keep
in Ferelden, to visit her husband.
Justice for Kristoff
is triggered when she confronts her husband's corpse possessed by
Justice
. After the encounter she becomes angry and leaves. She can be found in the
Chantry of our Lady Redeemer
in
Amaranthine
, in the top left chamber. Justice must be in the active party for her to appear.
If Aura is consoled, she will agree to wait a while longer to bury her husband's remains in exchange for seeing the
darkspawn
responsible for his death destroyed.
Quests
Justice for Kristoff

======================================================================
PAGE: Baizyl Harrowmont
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Baizyl_Harrowmont
======================================================================

“I'm a good man. I try to live by the
Paragons
' examples, but... it's not always easy.”
Character
Baizyl Harrowmont
Race
Dwarf
Gender
Male
Class
Warrior
Caste
Noble
Location
Orzammar Proving
Quests
A Paragon of Her Kind
A Lord's Trust: The First Task
Family
Pyral Harrowmont
(cousin)
Voice
Patrick J. Adams
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Baizyl Harrowmont
is a
noble
dwarf
fighting in the
Provings
and a cousin to
Pyral Harrowmont
as well as a member of
House Harrowmont
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
The Warden
will meet Baizyl in the
Proving Grounds
if he is siding with Harrowmont and completed
A Lord's Trust: The First Task
.
Dulin Forender
will task the Warden to find out why he along with
Gwiddon
, the other Harrowmont's fighter, dropped out of the Proving.
If the Warden cannot persuade Baizyl to tell his story, he will just sit out of the Proving. If he is convinced, he will explain that he is being blackmailed by one of
Bhelen Aeducan
's fighters,
Myaja
.
When he was younger, he was seeing a girl named Revelka
Aeducan
. However her family married her to someone from
House Bemot
in order to strengthen the bonds between the two
houses
, however they haven't stopped seeing each other. Myaja has in her possession love letters from Revelka to him.
The Warden can propose to retrieve those letters and do so by either lockpicking Myaja's door or stealing the key from her. The letters are in a locked chest in her room.
When the Warden hands in the letters, Baizyl is relieved that Revelka is safe and accepts to fight in Harrowmont's name again. During the Proving, the Warden will be able to ask him to fight at their side when needed.
If you ask how much the letters are worth to him, he'll offer 10
. With a high enough
Coercion
, the Warden can get 15
.
If Harrowmont is made
king
, Baizyl will help lead the surface army. However if he didn't help his cousin in the Provings he will mention that the palace sent a missive as his cousin—now king of
Orzammar
—is disappointed by Baizyl's lack of support, while an investigation will also take place.
On the occasion that the Warden places Prince Bhelen on the throne, Baizyl will later remark that he has been summoned to a meeting with the king, and that he's as good as dead. If Baizyl sat out and Prince Bhelen gains the throne then he is told he would not be executed. However
Renvil Harrowmont
in
Dragon Age II
mentions that he is the last surviving member of the house, which implies that Baizyl was still killed even if he sat out.
Quests
A Paragon of Her Kind
A Lord's Trust: The First Task

======================================================================
PAGE: Bandelor
CATEGORIES: Characters, Dragon Age: Origins characters
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Bandelor
======================================================================

“As Steward, I am a non-voting member. I am not permitted to voice opinions about the vote, although I am forming some about our members.”
Character
Bandelor
Title
Assembly Steward
Race
Dwarf
Gender
Male
Caste
Noble
Location
Chamber of the Assembly
,
Diamond Quarter
Quests
Seek out Steward Bandelor
Voice
H. Richard Greene
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Bandelor
is a
dwarven
lord
who presides over the
Assembly
of
Orzammar
during the events of the
Fifth Blight
. As the
Steward of the Assembly
, he is a non-voting member.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Upon first granted access to the city of Orzammar,
the Warden
is directed to the Assembly Steward as part of
Seek out Steward Bandelor
quest. After witnessing a tense meeting of the Assembly over the succession crisis, Bandelor properly greets the Warden. He explains the situation as well as some information about how the Assembly functions and can keep the Warden updated on the progress of the election. He also mentions the critical role that
Prince Bhelen Aeducan
and
Lord Pyral Harrowmont
have in order to break the current stalemate.
During the finale of
A Paragon of Her Kind
quest, when the Warden obtains the
crown
from either
Branka
or
Caridin
and returns back to the
Chamber of the Assembly
, the
deshyrs
will follow the wishes of the
Paragon
regarding the next
King of Orzammar
.
Quests
Seek out Steward Bandelor
Quotes
Bandelor can be asked how the election is looking at a certain moment. In several cases, his answer will vary depending on the player's choices. See
here
for more information.
───────
When the Warden hasn't completed a task for each candidate:
"At this point, they're stone for stone. I wouldn't put any coin on the outcome. It's still up in the air, though. One big surprise, and it could go either way."
───────
When the Warden has completed the
first task
for Bhelen:
"Well, Harrowmont's still holding his main supporters, but the whole
Helmi House
just turned against him. It's still up in the air, though. One big surprise, and it could go either way."
───────
When the Warden has completed the
second task
for Bhelen:
"Harrowmont started to pull ahead, but Bhelen got a huge surge of support when he announced
Jarvia
's death. It's still up in the air, though. One big surprise, and it could go either way."
───────
After a new king is coronated:
"Your role in this election will long be remembered, Warden."
Bugs
Bandelor will answer that it could go either way even if the Warden completes the
first
and the
second
task for Harrowmont.

“Another doomed soul come to drown their sorrows here, I see?”
Character
Bella
Title
Waitress
Race
Human
Gender
Female
Location
Tavern
,

Quests

Voice
Mika Simmons
Buy price
25%
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Bella
is a
tavern
waitress in
Redcliffe Village
. If a conversation is initiated with
the Warden
, she admits that she is receiving poor wages and being groped by
Lloyd
, the tavern owner. The Warden may have the option of helping her leave her life of poverty.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Note:
You can only do this quest when preparing Redcliffe for battle.
If the Warden intimidates
Lloyd
into joining the defense of Redcliffe Village, Bella takes over the tavern, giving the Warden access to free supplies (see bug below). If Lloyd lives through the battle, the "bug" still works and Bella, strangely, still has control of the business, even though Lloyd is once more behind the bar.
If you try to talk to Lloyd after he has given you his
ring
, he'll say "You put Bella in charge, talk to her"
(he will regain control and open for business as a
merchant
only after the Warden helps Bella leave Redcliffe).
After having successfully completed
A Village Under Siege
, the Warden can talk to Bella again. In this encounter, the Warden can give her 100
to "get out of Redcliffe" or 500
to "start a new life" (if the promise to help her has been made before the battle). She can alternatively take over the tavern if you force Lloyd to fight and he dies in the battle (the epilogue later notes she renames the tavern
The Warden's Rest
). She may also be persuaded to kiss a male Warden as thanks for saving the town. The Warden may also present Bella with an opportunity to be a maid for
Bann Teagan
.
For giving her 500
:
Wynne approves (
+8
) (
Wynne approves (
+4
) on
xbox360
Ultimate Edition),
Alistair approves (
+1
) and
Morrigan disapproves (
-10
) (successful persuasion will soften that to
-3
). The
epilogue
slideshow will note that Bella moved to
Denerim
and started her own brewery).
If the Warden suggests she become Bann Teagan's maid,
Morrigan disapproves (
-3
) and
Alistair approves (
+1
).
If the Warden accepts a kiss for saving the town, Leliana and/or Morrigan disapprove to the tune of
(
-10
) regardless of romance status.
Alistair approves (
+1
),
Oghren approves (
+2
) and also mutters "Hot." The three militiamen will just sit and stare with mouths hanging open.
If you kiss her twice during the conversation (because you survived the battle and in exchange for the money you give her), the approval with Oghren will go up by
(
+4
), while with a romanced Leliana, it could go down by
(
-20
).
Alternatively, if The Warden offers Bella the tavern, and picks the option "I didn't say for free...", she gives you 3
and agrees to pay you more later. This option is only available if you have intimidated Lloyd into giving you the tavern. The non-trading free inventory (see below) will shift from Lloyd to her.
If The Warden chooses to abandon Redcliffe, and leaves the village to fight for itself, Bella will die and come back as a revived corpse in the castle.

Notes
When
Shale
was moved from Redcliffe to
Honnleath
during development, a humorous exchange with Bella was left as a cut content. This exchange can still be found within Bella's dialogue file via the toolset.
Bella:
"Yes? Can I--oh dear. Isn't that the statue that's been standing outside for years, now?"
Shale:
"And it is the human that likes to lie in my shadow when coupling with others of its kind. Delightful."
Bella:
"And it speaks! Does it drink ale, too?"
Shale:
"Thankfully, no. Perhaps it should serve those who require serving?"
Bella:
"Then I will... do that. Welcome to the bar, at any rate, er... statue-man."
Bugs
ps3
xbox360
With
The Stone Prisoner
DLC installed, and after intimidating Lloyd into leaving and joining the defense of the town, Bella will allow you to look through Lloyd's tavern supplies and help yourself to the items he has in storage. After you receive crystals from her, leave the tavern and then walk back in. Bella will allow you to help yourself to the crystals again. Obviously, you can sell these crystals and have an infinite supply of gold. However, this bug, at least if overused, seems to lead to a corrupted save file, probably due to a counter overflow in tracking the total number of crystals sold to a merchant (PC was patched with v1.02).
ps3
To avoid save file corruption from over use of Bella's infinite sovereigns glitch, confine buying and selling the free crystals to the temporary merchant in Haven. His store is unique in that it does not keep track of all the crystals that you sell him, nor does his store remain after you initiate the events in the town. Or to be on the safe side, one could restrian doing this glitch until brilliant and flawless crystals appear in Bella's store (lvl 18-20) (Only confirmed by one user need another to verify). Flawless crystals available with lvl 16 character.
If Lloyd dies in
The Attack at Nightfall
and the Warden returns to the tavern, a male Warden can ask Bella for a kiss by selecting the "hero's welcome" dialogue option and/or in return for the 500
. During the cut-scene for either kiss Lloyd will appear behind the counter for a few seconds before the camera cuts away. Once the kiss is over Lloyd is again gone. Any romance-able female party member will lose approval if you accept the kiss, even if they are not being romanced.
pc
To trigger the option of giving Bella money it is necessary to talk to her during
A Village Under Siege
quest. Otherwise, there is no way of offering her money after
The Attack at Nightfall
is completed. (Tested for PC with v1.04)
When talking to her, select "So how has business been?"
You can offer to talk to Lloyd about her situation.
Be sure to follow through with "Why don't you leave?" and "You could leave if you had some help."
You should get her response to talk after the battle.
References

is an

who can be found loitering at

's

in

.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
During the quest
A Village Under Siege
Berwick can be conversed with, but he will quickly reveal that he is not very talkative and end the conversation unless:
The Warden
previously learned his name from either
Bella
or Lloyd and asks about it.
The Warden stole Berwick's
letter
already (which unlocks
Codex entry: Berwick's Letter
.
Leliana
or
Zevran
are in the party, either of whom will quickly reveal him as a spy.
Upon further investigation, the Warden will be told that the elf is waiting for his brother; however, since Berwick is very stressed, he can be persuaded or intimidated (Improved
Coercion
is required) to reveal that he was sent to spy on the
castle
by "a tall man" working for
Arl Howe
, who was
Teyrn Loghain
's right hand. However, he only managed to send word to his employers that
Arl Eamon
is sick, because afterwards the monsters came out of the castle and Berwick was stuck in the village.
Regardless of how he is exposed, the Warden receives Berwick's letter (unless it was obtained before) and then there are several options that can be followed:
The elf can be told to leave the village.
Berwick can be forced into helping defend the village. Doing so will make
Sten
say "Fitting" and gain
(
+2
). However,
Oghren disapproves (
-5
) unless a persuasion check is successful.
Berwick can be killed by the Warden. If the Warden is persuaded by
Oghren
that this is the appropriate course of action,
Oghren approves (
+4
).
If killed, Berwick may drop a
Splintmail
.

PAGE: Beyha Joam

“Those accusations are baseless, groundless, heinous, unsupportable, insubstantial... disregardable... Oh forget it. Get them!”
Character
Beyha Joam
Race
Human
Gender
Male
Class
Rogue
Location
Trade Route
,
Ferelden
Quests
Five Pages, Four Mages
Voice
Douglas Weston
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Beyha Joam
is a con artist who sells so-called "holy" documents.
Involvement
While visiting the
Circle Tower
during (or after) the
Broken Circle
quest, one can find a
Torn Page
that triggers the quest
Five Pages, Four Mages
. When all five pages are assembled, it is possible to stumble upon
Beyha Joam
in the
Trade Route
scripted
encounter
while traveling on the
world map
. The conversation inevitably ends in combat.
Quests
Five Pages, Four Mages
Strategy
Send a member of the party to attack the mage who sits on top of a little hill to the left (one may need to send all of their party members for him if they have no spells or abilities to counter mages), then kill the bandits Beyha has with him. The battle can be easy if the mage is taken out first, and may otherwise pose quite a challenge.
Loot
Various random items (including
Noble Clothing
)
Gold
Cameo Cowl
Quotes
"Ah, a fellow traveler of the fair lands. Are you a seeker, perchance? My packs are light, but I have a tome of strange origin: The Deus vi Eternus! Rumored to be the last message to a sinful world from the
Maker
Himself."
"I have no idea what you're talking about.. and neither do these large men carrying swords." *pause* "Get them!"
"Ah, will the wonders of this all-too-small a land never cease. Well, they will for you...Get them!"
Trivia
The book's title is Latin for "The Eternal God."

“We need absolute unity to fight against the fulcrum of
true evil
.”

)

:
